channel.title: Cobalt Shields - What We're Reading Today
channel.description: Cobalt Shields — Trusted | Reliable. Daily IT, cybersecurity, and infrastructure reading list curated for healthcare, higher ed, government, K-12, and nonprofit leaders.

image.title: Cobalt Shields - What We're Reading Today

item.title: Trump cancels AI executive order signing as California's Newsom signs worker-protection AI order
item.description: President Trump scrapped a planned Oval Office signing of a new federal AI order on May 22, telling reporters he did not like the text and worried regulation could blunt the U.S. lead over China. Hours later, California Governor Gavin Newsom signed a state executive order directing the labor department to study AI's impact on workers, expand unemployment insurance enrollment, and partner with UC and Stanford on public-interest AI. The split underscores a widening federal-state divide on AI governance that affects how higher-ed, healthcare, and government IT leaders plan procurement and workforce policy. Companion NPR coverage: https://www.npr.org/2026/05/22/g-s1-123671/ca-gov-gavin-newsom-signed-an-executive-order-to-protect-workers-from-ai | Source: NPR

item.title: DHS says ICE has 'no relationship' with spyware maker Paragon Solutions
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security told NPR on May 22 that Immigration and Customs Enforcement has no current contract or relationship with Israeli-founded spyware vendor Paragon Solutions, and that a previously revived contract was closed out on January 20. Advocates noted DHS declined to clarify whether ICE retains access to Paragon-developed tools through a third party, leaving open compliance questions under the 2023 executive order restricting commercial spyware. The episode highlights ongoing federal procurement, audit, and civil-liberties scrutiny around government use of mobile-device exploitation tools. | Source: NPR

item.title: Instructure strikes deal with ShinyHunters over Canvas hack; NWT government says ~1,700 employees and contractors affected
item.description: Instructure, parent of the Canvas learning management system, confirmed it reached an agreement with the ShinyHunters group claiming responsibility for the April–May breach affecting roughly 9,000 institutions worldwide. The company said it received assurance that no customers would be extorted as a result, but did not disclose whether payment was involved. Separately, the Government of the Northwest Territories disclosed that contact and training records for about 1,700 teachers, government employees, and contractors may have been exposed. Higher-ed, K-12, and public-sector IT teams that use Canvas should review contractual notification obligations and incident-response playbooks. NWT coverage: https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/north/canvas-breach-nwt-9.7208039 | Source: CBC News

item.title: Ottawa man charged in Canada and U.S. over Kimwolf botnet operation that infected millions of devices
item.description: Ontario Provincial Police arrested 23-year-old Jacob Butler of Ottawa, who is accused of helping develop and operate the Kimwolf botnet that, with Aisuru, infected millions of IoT devices including IPs on the U.S. Department of Defense Information Network. U.S. prosecutors say they tied Butler to the operation through IP addresses, online accounts, transaction records, and messaging logs obtained via legal process. The case follows the earlier disruption of the Kimwolf DDoS-for-hire service and underscores increasing law-enforcement focus on IoT-based botnets that disrupt government, healthcare, and education networks. | Source: CBC News

item.title: Saskatchewan nonprofit TeleMiracle / Kinsmen Foundation warns donors of possible data leak
item.description: The Kinsmen Foundation, which runs Saskatchewan's annual TeleMiracle telethon, told donors on May 22 that names, email addresses, phone numbers, residential addresses, and donation amounts may have been exposed in a breach of its systems. Saskatoon police are investigating and the foundation says systems have been secured with additional safeguards added. The incident is a reminder that even small-to-mid-size charities hold rich PII and require donor-CRM hardening and breach-notification playbooks. | Source: CBC News

item.title: U.S. tech lobby urges Ottawa to withdraw lawful access bill, citing encryption and trade risks
item.description: The Information Technology Industry Council — whose members include Amazon, Google, and Nvidia — formally called on the Canadian government to pull Bill C-22, arguing the lawful access regime has extraterritorial reach and creates conflict-of-law issues for global providers. Apple, Meta, Signal, and several VPN providers have publicly warned the bill threatens encryption guarantees; CSIS and the RCMP continue to lobby for passage by summer. The dispute is shaping cross-border tech procurement, encryption policy, and compliance planning for any Canadian healthcare, higher-ed, or government entity that relies on U.S. cloud or messaging providers. | Source: CBC News

item.title: Lawmakers warn U.S. data-broker location-protection rules leave White House, Congress, and CIA exposed
item.description: Senators Ron Wyden and Martin Heinrich and Representative Sara Jacobs warned the Trump administration that the April 2025 rules limiting commercial location-data sales to U.S. adversaries omit 736 sensitive locations including the White House, Congress, and CIA headquarters. The lawmakers say data brokers' sale of Americans' location data poses a serious national-security risk when government-employee movements can be mapped by foreign services. They urged a "protection zone" covering all of Washington, D.C. — a development federal contractors and any organization with sensitive personnel should track for compliance impact. | Source: PBS NewsHour

item.title: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes 120 flaws, no zero-days
item.description: Microsoft's May 2026 Patch Tuesday addresses 120 vulnerabilities including 29 critical RCE flaws. CVE-2026-41089 is a critical stack-based buffer overflow in Windows Netlogon granting SYSTEM privileges. No zero-days exploited in the wild — the first zero-day-free cycle since June 2024. | Source: BleepingComputer

item.title: Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day, the Sixth Exploited in 2026
item.description: CVE-2026-20182 is an authentication bypass in Cisco SD-WAN that lets remote attackers gain admin privileges. Exploited in targeted attacks by sophisticated threat actor UAT-8616. Sixth Cisco SD-WAN zero-day exploited this year. | Source: SecurityWeek

item.title: F5 Patches Over 50 Vulnerabilities in BIG-IP, BIG-IQ, and NGINX
item.description: F5's May 2026 quarterly security notification details 51 high and medium-severity vulnerabilities impacting BIG-IP, BIG-IQ, and NGINX. Administrators should prioritise patching internet-facing load balancers and application delivery controllers. | Source: SecurityWeek

item.title: CVE-2026-9082: Unauthenticated RCE in Drupal Under Active Exploitation
item.description: CVE-2026-9082 allows unauthenticated remote code execution, privilege escalation, and information disclosure in Drupal. Security firms are already tracking attacks against thousands of websites. Drupal has issued an emergency patch. | Source: SecurityWeek

item.title: Software Supply Chain Attack Targets Multiple PHP Laravel-Lang Packages
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers disclosed a fresh software supply chain attack campaign targeting multiple PHP packages belonging to Laravel-Lang. Affected packages and tags were published in rapid succession on May 22–23, 2026. Developers are advised to audit composer.lock files immediately. | Source: The Hacker News

item.title: Megalodon Campaign Pushes 5,718 Malicious Commits to GitHub Repositories in Six Hours
item.description: A new automated campaign called Megalodon pushed 5,718 malicious commits to 5,561 GitHub repositories within a six-hour window. The campaign targets CI/CD pipelines by injecting malicious workflow steps. GitHub has suspended the associated accounts. | Source: SecurityWeek

item.title: New Linux Malware "Showboat" Targets Post-Exploitation of Linux Systems
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers disclosed details of Showboat, a new Linux malware designed for post-exploitation. It spawns a remote shell, transfers files, and functions as a SOCKS5 proxy, making it well-suited for stealthy lateral movement inside enterprise environments. | Source: SecurityWeek

item.title: Canadian Man Arrested for Operating KimWolf DDoS Botnet Infecting Two Million Devices
item.description: U.S. and Canadian authorities arrested and charged a Canadian man accused of operating the KimWolf DDoS botnet, which allegedly infected nearly two million devices worldwide. The botnet was available as a DDoS-for-hire service. | Source: SecurityWeek

item.title: Palo Alto: Frontier AI Models Now Finding Majority of New CVEs in Patch Wednesday Advisories
item.description: Palo Alto Networks' May 2026 Defender's Guide reports that frontier AI models discovered the majority of the 26 CVEs in its latest Patch Wednesday advisories. AI is now operating at a speed and scale that fundamentally changes the vulnerability discovery landscape for both attackers and defenders. | Source: Palo Alto Networks

item.title: Microsoft's Multi-Model Agentic Security System Tops Leading Industry Benchmark
item.description: Microsoft's new multi-model agentic security system achieved 96% recall on 28 MSRC cases spanning five years in clfs.sys and 100% recall on 7 MSRC cases in tcpip.sys, topping industry benchmarks for AI-driven vulnerability triage. | Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: IBM Joins Project Glasswing with Anthropic to Protect Critical Software Infrastructure
item.description: IBM expanded its AI cybersecurity efforts by joining Project Glasswing, a security initiative with Anthropic and other technology firms. The move comes as security executives warn that frontier AI models have sharply accelerated attackers' ability to identify and exploit software vulnerabilities. | Source: IBM

item.title: Cognizant Launches Secure AI Services to Help Enterprises Scale Agentic Systems
item.description: Cognizant announced Secure AI Services, an integrated offering designed to help enterprises secure, govern, and scale AI and agentic systems. The launch addresses a significant gap: 63% of organisations cannot enforce purpose limitations on AI agents. | Source: Cognizant

item.title: May 2026 Patch Tuesday: 137 Vulnerabilities, No Zero-Days — First Clean Cycle in Years
item.description: SOCRadar's analysis of May 2026 Patch Tuesday covers 137 total vulnerabilities with 47% being elevation-of-privilege flaws. The absence of zero-days marks the first clean Patch Tuesday cycle since June 2024, giving security teams a window to catch up on backlogged patching. | Source: SOCRadar

item.title: Microsoft Ships Azure Linux 4.0 for Cloud AI Workloads
item.description: Microsoft announced Azure Linux 4.0 GA at Open Source Summit North America 2026. The Fedora-derived, RPM-based distribution is purpose-built for AI and cloud-native workloads on Azure, with hardened defaults and optimised kernel configurations for inference at scale. | Source: Let's Data Science

item.title: Open Source Summit NA 2026: AI Infrastructure, Supply Chain Security, and Agentic Systems Take Centre Stage
item.description: The Linux Foundation's 2026 summit in Minneapolis showcased the convergence of AI infrastructure, agentic systems, embedded Linux, and software supply chain security. Microsoft presented Azure Linux 4.0 alongside multiple open-source AI toolchain announcements. | Source: Linux Foundation

item.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition — Krebs on Security
item.description: Brian Krebs covers the May 2026 Patch Tuesday, highlighting the critical Netlogon vulnerability CVE-2026-41089, the significance of a zero-day-free cycle, and advice for prioritising the 29 critical RCE patches. Microsoft Office preview-pane exploitation vectors are called out as especially urgent. | Source: Krebs on Security

item.title: New Infosec Products of the Week: May 22, 2026
item.description: Help Net Security's weekly roundup includes Trust3 AI's MCP Security standard for safeguarding enterprise agentic AI, Babel Street's Insights Investigator for AI-assisted investigations, and several new SIEM and threat-intelligence integrations announced at RSA Conference 2026. | Source: Help Net Security

item.title: Supply Chain Attacks, AI Security, and Major Breaches Define Cybersecurity This Week in May 2026
item.description: eSecurity Planet's weekly roundup covers the Laravel-Lang supply chain attack, the Megalodon GitHub commit-injection campaign, ongoing Drupal exploitation, and the broader trend of AI-assisted offensive security tooling lowering the bar for sophisticated attacks against enterprise targets. | Source: eSecurity Planet
